QF.id: VI Public Offering and Corporate Actions_13

question.text: When does trading in Rights Entitlements on the secondary market close relative to the rights issue closure date?

answer.text: At least four days prior to the closure of the rights issue
On the closure date of the rights issue
Two days prior to the closure of the rights issue
Seven days prior to the closure of the rights issue

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Trading in REs on the secondary market shall commence along with the opening of the issue and be closed at least four days prior to the closure of the rights issue. **The Trap:** Two days is the common settlement gap, but four days is specified for RE trading closure. **Cross-Exam Context:** Rights issue timelines are tested in NISM Series VI and Series IX. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Investors who want to sell their REs must do so well before the rights issue closure — trading stops four days early.
